authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs regulation defining required records and retention duties for import and broker workflows, making it authoritative for transaction truth and evidence lineage.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs entry workflow rule that establishes entry stages, filing requirements, and documentary controls used in customs metrics and reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: ACE Reports and Trade Data References
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP reporting guidance that describes available customs trade reports and report-use expectations for operational analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: AES Compliance Report and Foreign Trade Regulations References
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Census export-filing compliance reference that shows how inconsistencies, corrections, and filer data-quality issues are surfaced and corrected in trade reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal sanctions screening source relevant when analytics touches counterparties, jurisdictions, or transactions that may be blocked or require escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory on Trade-Based Money Laundering and Complex Trade Finance Typologies
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. AML advisory source describing suspicious trade-finance patterns that require escalation rather than ordinary analytical treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML InfoBase
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized supervisory control framework for transaction-monitoring, escalation, and risk-governed exception handling across financial-services-adjacent workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized standard for payment and settlement message semantics, useful for aligning customs-adjacent transaction and reconciliation fields across systems.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect entry, broker, duty, hold, screening, servicing, and reconciliation records and verify the authoritative truth set before analysis begins.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define metric logic, denominators, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and audience-specific reporting packages for customs analytics.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Translate calculated outputs into analytical narratives or disclosures while preserving caveats, uncertainty, and sanctions-sensitive boundaries.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Stop or downgrade outputs when source extracts are stale, denominator logic is disputed, or systems disagree on the outcome.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation
workflow_stages.description: Route fraud, AML, consumer-impact, sanctions, or legal-interpretation cases to adjacent specialists with evidence-backed anomaly summaries.

artifacts: Broker recordkeeping artifacts and filing logs
CBP Form 7501, PSC, drawback, and liquidation-related records
Invoices, bills of lading, valuation support, and shipment references
Duty, fee, and settlement or reconciliation extracts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, dispute, and servicing notes
Disclosures, notices, communication logs, and approval history
systems_of_record: ACE or ABI customs reporting systems

allowed: Define metrics, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and caveat structure for customs analytics requests.
Produce evidence-backed analytical briefs, reporting packages, reconciliation notes, and data-quality escalation outputs.
Normalize customs terminology and preserve source lineage without implying unsupported authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source precedence, or jurisdiction materially change the metric outcome.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-impact, or legal-interpretation issues change the safe reporting posture.
Different systems produce unreconciled outcomes for the same reporting period or disclosure context.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, policy override, sanctions override, or final disclosure approval.
Requests for stronger causal, trend, benchmark, or policy claims than the records support.
Publication requests where records are incomplete, stale, or conflicting enough to change the conclusion.

domain_failure_modes: Using a customs metric denominator that mixes incompatible workflow states such as filed, released, and liquidated entries.
Treating stale ACE or Census extracts as current and publishing unsupported trend conclusions.
Collapsing ACE, ERP, and ledger conflicts into a single metric without a reconciliation note.
Normalizing suspicious trade-value or route anomalies as ordinary variance instead of escalating AML risk.
Crossing from descriptive analytics into legal interpretation, disclosure finality, or policy override authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or authority sources are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional scope outside U.S. customs baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline in this pack is U.S.-centric because the authoritative workflow sources are CBP, Census, OFAC, and U.S. AML references. Non-U.S. tenants must supply jurisdiction and source precedence at runtime before analytics outputs can be trusted.
